2605.10843.title: Training-Free Cultural Alignment of Large Language Models via Persona Disagreement
2605.10843.paper_summary: Large language models increasingly mediate decisions that turn on moral judgement, yet a growing body of evidence shows that their implicit preferences are not culturally neutral. Existing cultural alignment methods either require per-country preference data and fine-tuning budgets or assume white-box access to model internals that commercial APIs do not expose. In this work, we focus on this realistic black-box, public-data-only regime and observe that within-country sociodemographic disagreement, not consensus, is the primary steering signal. We introduce DISCA (Disagreement-Informed Steering for Cultural Alignment), an inference-time method that instantiates each country as a panel of World-Values-Survey-grounded persona agents and converts their disagreement into a bounded, loss-averse logit correction. Across 20 countries and 7 open-weight backbones (2B--70B), DISCA reduces cultural misalignment on MultiTP by 10--24% on the six backbones >=3.8B, and 2--7% on open-ended scenarios, without changing any weights. Our results suggest that inference-time calibration is a scalable alternative to fine-tuning for serving the long tail of global moral preferences.
2605.10843.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地介入道德判斷的決策，但越來越多的證據顯示，它們的隱含偏好並不是文化中立的。現有的文化對齊方法要麼需要每個國家的偏好數據和微調預算，要麼假設對模型內部的白盒訪問，而商業API並不公開這些信息。在這項工作中，我們專注於這種現實的黑盒、僅使用公共數據的模式，並觀察到國內社會人口學的不一致性，而非共識，才是主要的引導信號。我們引入了DISCA（基於不一致性的信息引導文化對齊），這是一種推理時的方法，將每個國家實例化為一組基於世界價值調查的角色代理，並將它們的分歧轉換為有界的、厭惡損失的邏輯修正。在20個國家和7個開放權重骨幹（2B--70B）中，DISCA在六個權重>=3.8B的MultiTP上減少了10--24%的文化錯位，在開放式場景中減少了2--7%，而不改變任何權重。我們的結果表明，推理時的校準是一種可擴展的替代方案，用於為全球道德偏好的長尾服務。
2605.10843.author: Huynh Trung Kiet et.al.
2605.10843.authors: Huynh Trung Kiet, Dao Sy Duy Minh, Tuan Nguyen, Chi-Nguyen Tran, Phu-Hoa Pham, Nguyen Lam Phu Quy, The Anh Han, Long Tran-Thanh
